behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: This failure typically appears at module handoff boundaries (manifest parser -> dependency resolver -> query input builder). Unit tests on each function can miss dropped transitive deps across wiring. No real network/OS primitive or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components together on fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct + transitive deps; assert the vulnerability-check input set includes every expected package@version and no omissions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real external API behavior/contract for version-range matching, not just local query-building logic. A stub can hide mismatches in how the real service interprets ranges.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real vulnerability API, submit manifests with same package name but different version ranges and verify returned CVEs differ appropriately by range; confirm no false positives from name-only matching.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in one boundary (comparator/sort function). Higher-level tests add little new reality signal beyond unit coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE fixtures into the sort function and assert exact output order by severity rank then CVSS descending within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: This is about parser/editor/serializer interaction and round-trip manifest rewrite correctness. Unit tests for a single edit helper can miss formatting/key-preservation behavior across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end manifest rewrite pipeline on realistic files; assert only the targeted dependency version changed and all other entries/structure remain byte- or semantic-equivalent as required.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible side effect on a real external boundary (repository/PR API) with readback of resulting diff. Mocks cannot validate real API contract or side-effect visibility.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a test repository, verify a real PR is opened, then fetch the PR from the hosting service and assert the diff contains exactly one dependency bump and no unrelated changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic; no external infra or multi-module reality check is inherently required.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparison function with representative versions (including pre-release/build metadata cases if supported) and assert breaking-change classification for major vs non-major bumps.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff: semver decision gates PR module invocation and report/output module state. A unit test per function can miss the combined behavior; real external API is not strictly required for this specific check.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with a known breaking bump candidate, using an in-process PR client fake and real reporting path; assert PR creation path is not invoked and output contains the review-required flag.
